name: S-K Bus Solutions Inc Which
recent_news: No public news identified; the suffix 'Which' in the name suggests truncated California SOS DBA filing ('Which Will Do Business As'). No website, phone, or news presence found.
fit_notes: Micro-operator in Redding/Shasta County CA with ~36 employees and ~$1.4M revenue; minimal online footprint suggests small family-run school transport. Possible tuck-in only if local district relationships exist; size below typical platform threshold.

name: United Bus Svc LLC
recent_news: FMCSA-registered April 2021 (DOT #3605042) as intrastate non-hazmat private passenger carrier with 8 power units; classified as private (non-business) passenger, NOT authorized for-hire interstate. No explicit school bus designation in SAFER snapshot.
fit_notes: Tiny 8-bus intrastate passenger carrier in Saint Paul; FMCSA records show 0 drivers logged and private (non-business) classification, raising doubt about active school-bus contracting. Likely too small and unclear service mix for a Summit tuck-in.

name: Flores School Van
recent_news: No public news found. Distinct from larger Houston-area Flores-name carriers (Autobuses Flores, Flores US Bus Lines Inc DOT #1776152, Flores Transportation on McKamy Dr) which operate at different addresses. Listed owner Blanca E Flores at 5303 Castlecreek Ln, Houston 77053.
fit_notes: Apparent home-address single-owner van operator in south Houston; if active in HISD/Aldine SPED or alt-transport segment could be a micro-tuck-in, but scale (~$1.4M, residential HQ) is sub-threshold and overlap with similarly-named carriers creates identification risk.

name: Busy Bus USA
recent_news: No public news, website, or FMCSA hit identified at the 6284 S Galena Way, Englewood 80111 address. Not to be confused with 'BusyBusy' (construction time-tracking software headquartered nearby in Englewood); no evidence of school-bus operations.
fit_notes: Residential-address Englewood entity with no discoverable operating footprint; likely too small/unverifiable as a true student-transport contractor. Not a credible Summit tuck-in candidate without on-the-ground confirmation.

name: Amestoy Corp Which Will Do Bus
recent_news: No direct news on a bus operation. CA SOS shows an 'Amestoy Construction Corp' at Valley Springs (incorporated May 2000, principal Mark Thomas Amestoy) at a similar locale; the listed name 'Amestoy Corp Which Will Do Bus[iness as]' appears to be a truncated California SOS DBA filing rather than confirmation of bus operations. No FMCSA/school-bus footprint identified.
fit_notes: Likely mis-classified: the only verifiable 'Amestoy Corp' in Valley Springs is a single-family-home construction GC, not a school bus operator. Calaveras USD appears to run district-owned transportation. Not a viable Summit tuck-in target.
